g.text: sql | 'CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL)'
rootpage=3 → users B-tree root is page 3
rootpage=7 → idx_email B+tree root is page 7
Page 1 = sqlite_master root — FIXED by convention, no catalog needed to find it
sqlite_master stores schema in the SAME B-tree format as all user tables
Schema IS data: schema_find_table() reads the catalog like any other SELECT
Circular dependency resolved: page 1 is the bootstrap entry point
0x000–0x063 | 100 bytes | DB file header (magic, page_size, enc)
...–0xFFF | variable | cell content area (records grow upward)
Always page 1 — known without any catalog lookup
page N | TABLE_LEAF or TABLE_INTERNAL | user table B-tree
page M | INDEX_LEAF or INDEX_INTERNAL | secondary index B+tree
rootpage column in sqlite_master points here
name | TEXT | table or index name (e.g. 'users')
tbl_name | TEXT | owning table (same as name for tables)
rootpage | INTEGER | B-tree root page number in database file
sql | TEXT | original CREATE TABLE / CREATE INDEX SQL
One row per table, one row per index
TABLE_LEAF Cell Wire Format (each sqlite_master row)
payload_size | varint | total byte count of the record payload
rowid | varint | auto-assigned integer row ID
header_size | varint | byte count of header region
serial_type0 | varint | type code for 'type' column (TEXT)
serial_type1 | varint | type code for 'name' column (TEXT)
... | varints | serial_types for tbl_name, rootpage, sql
col_data | bytes | packed big-endian column values
Serial type = 2*len+13 for TEXT, 1–6 for INTEGER
columns[] | ColumnDef* | name, type, NOT NULL, PK flags
Populated by schema_load() at db_open time
4. In-memory schema map ready — queries can execute
2. Allocate new root page for the table
